methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $14M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Starpath Robotics is a Seed-stage company in Space & Aerospace that has raised $14.5M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and a broader private cohort including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace. However, the narrow peer set for Seed-stage Space & Aerospace companies yielded insufficient datapoints for robust median-based triangulation.

Stage-progression analysis, grounded in the $14.5M total raised as a proxy baseline, applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-round step-ups in this sector. This method yields an implied valuation of $36M. Public-comp anchoring using the 6-company Space & Aerospace P/S median of 45.3x cannot be applied, as the target's revenue is not disclosed—standard for private companies at this maturity. Peer-median analysis similarly lacks the three minimum same-or-adjacent-stage comparables required for statistical confidence.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $31M to $42M, centered on $36M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the target's actual revenue run rate (needed to anchor public multiples), the stage-progression multiplier's appropriateness given Starpath's specific technology and market position, and the availability of tighter private peer data from recent Space & Aerospace Seed rounds. Further diligence on unit economics and capital efficiency would strengthen conviction.
